question: What is the capital of France?
note: Paris has been France's capital for over 1,000 years.

question: What is the capital of Australia?
note: Canberra became the capital in 1913 as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

question: What is the capital of Japan?
note: Tokyo has been Japan's capital since 1869.

question: Name the capital of Brazil.
note: Brasília replaced Rio de Janeiro as Brazil's capital in 1960.

question: What is the capital of Canada?
note: Ottawa has been Canada's capital since 1857, chosen by Queen Victoria.

question: What is the capital of Germany?
note: Berlin became the reunified Germany's capital again in 1990.

question: What is the capital of Australia?
note: Canberra became the capital in 1913.

question: What is the capital of South Korea?
note: Seoul is home to about half of South Korea's entire population.

question: What is the capital of Spain?
note: Madrid became Spain's capital in 1561 under King Philip II.

question: What is the capital of Italy?
note: Rome has been Italy's capital since 1871.

question: What is the capital of Argentina?
note: Buenos Aires means 'Fair Winds' in Spanish.

question: What is the capital of Egypt?
note: Cairo is Africa's largest city with over 20 million people in its metro area.

question: What is the capital of Mexico?
note: Mexico City is built on the ruins of Tenochtitlan.

question: What is the capital of India?
note: New Delhi was designed by British architect Edwin Lutyens.

question: What is the capital of China?
note: Beijing has served as China's capital for most of the last 700 years.

question: What is the capital of Russia?
note: Moscow has been Russia's capital since 1918.

question: What is the capital of Turkey?
note: Ankara became Turkey's capital in 1923.

question: What is the capital of Sweden?
note: Stockholm was founded in 1252 and is built across 14 islands.

question: What is the capital of Norway?
note: Oslo has been Norway's capital since 1814.

question: What is the capital of Finland?
note: Helsinki became Finland's capital in 1812.

question: What is the capital of Denmark?
note: Copenhagen has been Denmark's capital since the early 15th century.

question: What is the capital of the Netherlands?
note: Amsterdam is the constitutional capital; The Hague is the seat of government.

question: What is the capital of Portugal?
note: Lisbon is the westernmost capital of continental Europe.

question: What is the capital of Greece?
note: Athens has been continuously inhabited for over 3,000 years.

question: What is the capital of Poland?
note: Warsaw rebuilt almost completely after WWII — known as the 'Phoenix City'.

question: What is the capital of Ukraine?
note: Kyiv is one of Europe's oldest cities, founded in the 5th century AD.

question: What is the capital of Saudi Arabia?
note: Riyadh means 'The Gardens' in Arabic.

question: What is the capital of Thailand?
note: Bangkok's full ceremonial name is the longest place name in the world.

question: What is the capital of Vietnam?
note: Hanoi has been Vietnam's capital since 1010.

question: What is the capital of New Zealand?
note: Wellington has been New Zealand's capital since 1865 — Auckland is the largest city.

question: What is the capital of South Africa?
note: South Africa has three capitals — Pretoria (executive), Cape Town (legislative), Bloemfontein (judicial).

question: What is the capital of Kazakhstan?
note: Astana (formerly Nur-Sultan) replaced Almaty as capital in 1997.

question: What is the capital of Pakistan?
note: Islamabad replaced Karachi as Pakistan's capital in 1966.

question: What is the capital of Kenya?
note: Nairobi was founded in 1899 as a railway camp.

question: What is the capital of Nigeria?
note: Abuja replaced Lagos as Nigeria's capital in 1991.

question: What is the capital of Morocco?
note: Rabat has been Morocco's capital since 1912 — Casablanca is the largest city but not the capital.

question: What is the capital of Peru?
note: Lima was founded by Spanish conquistador Francisco Pizarro in 1535.

question: What is the capital of Colombia?
note: Bogotá sits at 2,600m above sea level.

question: What is the capital of Iraq?
note: Baghdad was founded in 762 AD and was once the world's largest city.

question: What is the capital of Jordan?
note: Amman is built on seven hills and was called Philadelphia in antiquity.

question: What is the capital of the UAE?
note: Abu Dhabi is the UAE's capital — Dubai is the largest city and financial hub.

question: What is the capital of Qatar?
note: Doha hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup.

question: What is the capital of Bangladesh?
note: Dhaka is one of the world's most densely populated cities.

question: What is the capital of Myanmar?
note: Naypyidaw replaced Yangon as Myanmar's capital in 2006.

question: What is the capital of Ghana?
note: Accra became Ghana's capital under British colonial rule.

question: What is the capital of Tanzania?
note: Dodoma is Tanzania's official capital since 1996; Dar es Salaam is the largest city.

question: What is the capital of Cuba?
note: Havana was founded in 1519 and is famous for its preserved colonial architecture.

question: What is the capital of Iceland?
note: Reykjavík is the world's northernmost national capital.

question: What is the capital of Hungary?
note: Budapest was formed in 1873 by merging Buda, Óbuda, and Pest.

question: What is the capital of Czech Republic?
note: Prague Castle is the largest ancient castle complex in the world.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Japan's Nisshōki — the red sun disc on white — dates to the 7th century.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Canada's red maple leaf flag was adopted on 15 February 1965.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Colombia's yellow, blue and red tricolour — legacy of Gran Colombia.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Germany's black, red, gold tricolour was adopted by the Frankfurt Parliament in 1848.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Denmark's Dannebrog — white cross on red — is one of the world's oldest flags, dating to 1219.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: France's vertical blue, white, red tricolour was adopted during the Revolution in 1794.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Argentina's flag features the 'Sol de Mayo' — a sun with a human face — adopted in 1818.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Australia's flag has the Union Jack, Commonwealth Star, and Southern Cross.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Switzerland's square white cross on red is one of only two square national flags.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Brazil's flag features the 'Ordem e Progresso' motto and 27 stars.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Pakistan's dark green with white crescent and star — adopted in 1947.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Turkey's white crescent and star on red — adopted in 1844.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: The Netherlands flag is horizontal red-white-blue, dating from 1572.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: South Africa's six-colour flag was adopted in 1994 to mark the end of apartheid.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: India's saffron, white and green with the blue Ashoka Chakra — adopted 1947.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Thailand's red, white and blue horizontal stripes — adopted 1917.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Bhutan's orange and yellow flag features the Druk — the Thunder Dragon.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Lebanon's flag features the Cedar of Lebanon, a symbol of immortality.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Kenya's flag features a Maasai shield and spears on black, red and green.

question: Which country does this flag belong to?
note: Iceland's blue, white and red Nordic cross — adopted 1944.

question: Which country has a red circle on a white background?
note: Japan's Nisshōki — the rising sun flag — has been in use since the 7th century.

question: Which country's flag features a maple leaf?
note: Canada's red maple leaf flag was adopted in 1965.

question: Which country has a dragon on its flag?
note: Bhutan's Druk (Thunder Dragon) gives the country its local name 'Druk Yul'.

question: Which country's flag features the Ashoka Chakra (a wheel with 24 spokes)?
note: India's navy blue Ashoka Chakra sits on the white band between saffron and green.

question: Which country has an AK-47 on its national flag?
note: Mozambique's flag is the only national flag featuring a modern assault rifle.

question: What colour is the cross on Finland's flag?
note: Finland's blue Nordic cross on white — adopted in 1918.

question: What colour is the cross on Sweden's flag?
note: Sweden's yellow/gold Nordic cross on blue dates from at least the 16th century.

question: What colour is the background of Japan's flag?
note: Japan's Hinomaru — a red disc on white.

question: What colour is the background of Pakistan's flag?
note: Pakistan's flag is predominantly dark green with a white vertical stripe.

question: What colour are the stars on China's national flag?
note: China's red flag has one large and four smaller yellow stars.

question: What colour is the background of China's national flag?
note: China's flag is red with five yellow stars — adopted in 1949.

question: What are the three colours of the Italian flag?
note: Italy's vertical tricolour: green, white, red — adopted in 1797.

question: What are the colours of the German flag?
note: Germany's tricolour — black, red, gold — adopted by the Frankfurt Parliament in 1848.

question: What three colours appear on the Russian flag?
note: Russia's white-blue-red tricolour was introduced by Peter the Great in 1699.

question: What colour is the background of Turkey's flag?
note: Turkey's flag is a white crescent and star on a red background.

question: What colours make up the South Korean flag (Taegukgi)?
answers: White background with red, blue and black
note: South Korea's Taegukgi has a white background with the Taiji symbol and four black trigrams.

question: What are the Pan-African colours found on many African flags?
note: Red, Black and Green were chosen by Marcus Garvey's Pan-African movement in 1920.

question: Which country does NOT share a land border with France?
note: Portugal only borders Spain. France has 8 land neighbours.

question: Which country borders BOTH China and India?
note: Nepal sits between China (Tibet) to the north and India to the south.

question: How many countries does Germany border?
note: Germany borders Denmark, Netherlands, Belgium, Luxembourg, France, Switzerland, Austria, Czech Republic, and Poland.

question: How many countries does Brazil share a land border with?
note: Brazil borders 10 countries — every South American nation except Chile and Ecuador.

question: Which country is completely surrounded by South Africa?
note: Lesotho is entirely enclosed within South Africa.

question: Which country is completely surrounded by Italy?
note: Vatican City is entirely enclosed within Rome.

question: Which country is completely landlocked within South Africa?
note: Lesotho is the only country in the world entirely surrounded by one other country.

question: Which country borders both Norway AND Russia?
note: Finland shares borders with Norway, Sweden, and Russia.

question: Which country does NOT border the Mediterranean Sea?
note: Jordan is landlocked and does not touch the Mediterranean — it has a small Red Sea coastline.

question: How many countries does China share a land border with?
note: China and Russia both share land borders with 14 countries — the most of any nation.

question: Which country borders the most other countries?
note: Russia and China are tied with 14 land borders each.

question: Which two countries share the world's longest international border?
note: The US-Canada border is the world's longest at 8,891 km.

question: Which country does NOT border Germany?
note: Wait — Luxembourg does border Germany. The trick answer: the Netherlands borders Germany but not all expect it.

question: Which African country is completely surrounded by Senegal (except for its coast)?
note: The Gambia is almost entirely surrounded by Senegal — one of the world's most unusual borders.

question: Which is the only country to border both the Caspian Sea and the Persian Gulf?
note: Iran has coastlines on both the Caspian Sea (north) and Persian Gulf (south).

question: What is the name of the strait between Spain and Morocco?
note: The Strait of Gibraltar is only 14 km wide at its narrowest point.

question: Which country does NOT border the Black Sea?
note: Syria has no Black Sea coastline — it borders only the Mediterranean.

question: Which three countries border Luxembourg?
note: Luxembourg is bordered by France to the south, Belgium to the west, and Germany to the east.

question: Which ocean lies between Europe and North America?
note: The Atlantic was named after the Titan Atlas in Greek mythology.

question: Which continent is Egypt on?
note: Egypt is in northeastern Africa, though the Sinai Peninsula touches Asia.

question: What is the world's largest country by land area?
note: Russia covers 17.1 million km² — nearly twice the size of Canada.

question: What is the longest river in the world?
note: The Nile runs approximately 6,650 km through northeastern Africa.

question: What is the world's largest ocean?
note: The Pacific covers about 165 million km² — more than all land combined.

question: What is the largest desert in the world?
note: Antarctica is the world's largest desert at 14 million km². The Sahara is the largest hot desert.

question: Which is the largest continent?
note: Asia covers about 44 million km² — nearly 30% of Earth's land area.

question: What is the largest country in Africa by area?
note: Algeria covers 2.38 million km² — the largest country in Africa.

question: Which country has the most time zones?
note: France spans 12 time zones due to overseas territories.

question: What is the world's smallest country?
note: Vatican City covers just 0.44 km².

question: What is the deepest lake in the world?
note: Lake Baikal in Russia is 1,642m deep and contains 20% of the world's fresh surface water.

question: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?
note: The Ural Mountains form a natural boundary between European and Asian Russia.

question: What is the longest river in Africa?
note: The Nile runs ~6,650 km to the Mediterranean.

question: What is the largest lake in Africa?
note: Lake Victoria is Africa's largest lake and the world's largest tropical lake.

question: Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
note: Canada has a coastline of 202,080 km — the world's longest, including islands.

question: What is the world's largest island?
note: Greenland is 2.16 million km² — though an autonomous territory of Denmark.

question: What is the name of the sea between Italy and Croatia?
note: The Adriatic Sea separates the Italian Peninsula from the Balkans.

question: Which country contains Angel Falls, the world's highest waterfall?
note: Angel Falls in Venezuela drops 979m — nearly 20 times the height of Niagara Falls.

question: What is the name of the channel between England and France?
note: The English Channel is 34 km at its narrowest at the Strait of Dover.

question: What is the highest mountain in Africa?
note: Kilimanjaro in Tanzania is 5,895m — Africa's highest peak.

question: In which ocean is the island of Madagascar?
note: Madagascar lies in the Indian Ocean, about 400 km off the coast of Mozambique.

question: What is the longest mountain range in the world?
note: The Andes run 7,000 km along South America's western coast.

question: What is the name of the largest ocean between Africa and Australia?
note: The Indian Ocean is the third largest ocean, covering about 70 million km².

question: Which strait separates Asia from North America?
note: The Bering Strait is only 85 km wide and was a land bridge during the Ice Age.

question: What is the name of the sea between Saudi Arabia and Africa?
note: The Red Sea connects to the Mediterranean via the Suez Canal.

question: What currency is used in Japan?
note: The Japanese Yen (¥) has been in use since 1871.

question: What currency is used in the UK?
note: The British pound is the world's oldest currency still in use, over 1,200 years old.

question: What currency does Switzerland use?
note: Switzerland never adopted the Euro despite being surrounded by Eurozone countries.

question: What currency is used in South Korea?
note: The South Korean Won (KRW) has been used since 1945.

question: What currency is used in Brazil?
note: The Brazilian Real replaced the cruzeiro in 1994.

question: What currency is used in Russia?
note: The Russian Ruble dates to the 13th century.

question: What currency does Poland use?
note: The Polish Złoty (PLN) has been Poland's currency since 1924.

question: Which of these countries does NOT use the Euro?
note: Bulgaria uses the Lev (BGN) — an EU member but not yet in the Eurozone.

question: What currency is used in India?
note: The Indian Rupee (₹) symbol was officially adopted in 2010.

question: What currency is used in Turkey?
note: The Turkish Lira has been used since 1923.

question: What currency is used in Norway?
note: The Norwegian Krone (NOK) — Norway chose not to join the Eurozone.

question: What is the currency of Israel?
note: The Israeli New Shekel (ILS) replaced the old Shekel in 1986.

question: What currency is used in South Africa?
note: The South African Rand (ZAR) is named after the Witwatersrand gold-bearing reef.

question: What is the currency of Hungary?
note: Hungary uses the Forint (HUF) — it has chosen not to adopt the Euro.

question: What is the currency of Czech Republic?
note: The Czech Koruna (CZK) replaced the Czechoslovak Koruna in 1993.

question: Which country has the international dialling code +44?
note: The UK's +44 was assigned in 1960 when international dialling was introduced.

question: Which country has the dialling code +1?
note: Both the USA and Canada share +1 — the North American Numbering Plan.

question: Which country has the dialling code +61?
note: Australia's +61. New Zealand is +64, South Africa is +27.

question: Which country has the dialling code +49?
note: Germany's +49. Austria is +43, Switzerland is +41.

question: Which country has the dialling code +81?
note: Japan's +81 was assigned in 1960.

question: Which country has the dialling code +7?
note: Russia uses +7. Kazakhstan also uses +7 — the only two countries sharing this code.

question: Which country has the dialling code +354?
note: Iceland's +354 — one of the trickiest dial codes to remember.

question: Which country has the dialling code +91?
note: India's +91. Pakistan is +92, Bangladesh is +880.

question: Which country has the dialling code +55?
note: Brazil's +55. Argentina is +54.

question: Which country has the dialling code +82?
note: South Korea's +82. Japan is +81, China is +86.

question: What is Finland's country-code internet domain? (include the dot)
note: Finland's .fi domain has been registered since 1986.

question: Which country uses the internet TLD .de?
note: Germany's .de (Deutschland) is the world's most used country code TLD.

question: What country uses the TLD .au?
note: Australia's .au. Austria uses .at, Azerbaijan uses .az.

question: Which country uses the internet TLD .za?
note: .za comes from Suid-Afrika, the Afrikaans name for South Africa.

question: What does the TLD .io originally stand for?
note: The .io is the country code for British Indian Ocean Territory, now popular with tech companies.

question: How many official languages does Switzerland have?
note: German, French, Italian, and Romansh.

question: What is the official language of Brazil?
note: Brazil is the only Portuguese-speaking country in South America.

question: Which country has the most official languages?
note: South Africa has 11 official languages.

question: How many official languages does Belgium have?
note: Dutch, French, and German.

question: What language do people in Iceland speak?
note: Icelandic has barely changed in 1,000 years — modern Icelanders can read Viking sagas.

question: Which African language is official in both Kenya and Tanzania?
note: Swahili (Kiswahili) is co-official with English in both countries.

question: What is the official language of Egypt?
note: Modern Standard Arabic is Egypt's official language.

question: How many official languages does the EU have?
note: The EU has 24 official languages, with German the most widely spoken native language.

question: How many official languages does South Africa have?
note: South Africa has 11 official languages including Zulu, Xhosa, Afrikaans, and English.

question: What is the national animal of Australia?
note: The red kangaroo appears on Australia's coat of arms alongside the emu.

question: What is the national animal of Canada?
note: The Canadian beaver has been an official emblem since 1975.

question: What is the national animal of Scotland?
note: The unicorn has been Scotland's national animal since the 12th century.

question: Which country is Sushi originally from?
note: Sushi originated in Japan, evolving from a method of preserving fish in fermented rice.

question: Which country is Paella originally from?
note: Paella originated in Valencia, Spain, in the 19th century.

question: Injera flatbread is the national dish of which country?
note: Injera is made from teff grain and serves as both bread and eating utensil.

question: Which country invented Kimchi?
note: Kimchi has been made in Korea for thousands of years.

question: Which country is the world's largest coffee producer?
note: Brazil produces about 35% of the world's coffee supply.

question: In which country did the Tango originate?
note: Tango originated in the working-class areas of Buenos Aires in the late 19th century.

question: What is the national dish of Scotland?
note: Haggis — made from sheep offal mixed with oatmeal — is Scotland's national dish.

question: Which country is the world's largest producer of olive oil?
note: Spain produces about 45% of the world's olive oil.

question: Which country is the world's largest tea producer?
note: China produces about 45% of the world's tea.

question: Which country is home to the ancient ruins of Machu Picchu?
note: Machu Picchu was built by the Inca Empire in the 15th century.

question: What is the world's largest economy by GDP?
note: The US GDP is about $27 trillion — the world's largest.

question: Which country has the world's largest sovereign wealth fund?
note: Norway's Government Pension Fund Global is worth over $1.7 trillion.

question: Which country is the world's largest exporter of goods?
note: China became the world's largest goods exporter in 2009.

question: In which country is IKEA originally from?
note: IKEA was founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad in Älmhult, Sweden.

question: Which country is H&M originally from?
note: H&M was founded in 1947 in Västerås, Sweden.

question: In which country is the Toyota company headquartered?
note: Toyota is headquartered in Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture, Japan.

question: Which country is Samsung from?
note: Samsung is headquartered in Suwon, South Korea.

question: Which country is the world's largest producer of cocoa?
note: Ivory Coast produces about 45% of the world's cocoa.

question: Which country spans the most time zones?
note: France spans 12 time zones due to overseas territories.

question: Which is the only country that borders both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans?
note: Colombia is the only South American country with coastlines on both oceans.

question: Which is the only Arab country to have hosted the FIFA World Cup?
note: Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup.

question: Which country has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
note: Italy leads with 58 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

question: Which is the world's newest internationally recognised country?
note: South Sudan gained independence on 9 July 2011.

question: Which country has the most natural lakes?
note: Canada has about 879,800 lakes — more than any other country.

question: Which country was the first to give women the right to vote?
note: New Zealand became the first self-governing country to grant women's suffrage, in 1893.

question: Papua New Guinea has the most of what compared to any country?
note: Papua New Guinea has over 800 languages — the most linguistic diversity of any country.

question: Which country produces 90% of the world's saffron?
note: Iran produces about 90% of the world's saffron — the world's most expensive spice by weight.

question: Which country has the world's highest average elevation?
note: Among recognised countries, Bhutan has the highest average elevation at about 3,280m.
